tasks.title: Domain: renderQrSvg(text) — async SVG renderer over qrcode
tasks.dod: new module src/qr.js exports renderQrSvg(text) returning a Promise of an SVG string via QRCode.toString(text, { type: 'svg' }); qrcode ^1.5 added to dependencies per ADR-0001; the module imports no express and no better-sqlite3, and src/shorten.js still imports only node:crypto; tests pin that the render is deterministic for a given text and that the payload is not echoed into the markup

tasks.title: App: GET /api/qr/:code — svg body, content-type, cache header, 404/410
tasks.dod: the 501 stub is replaced by an async handler that probes the store with getStats (never resolveLink), answers 404 { error: 'not found' } for an unknown code, renders renderQrSvg(`${req.protocol}://${req.get('host')}/${code}`) inside a try/catch whose catch calls next(err), and sends the svg with res.type('svg') plus Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable; the route stays above the catch-all GET /:code; clicks is unchanged by any number of requests; the 410 branch is written only once link-expiry is shipped

tasks.title: Frontend: QR button in the links table
tasks.dod: each row of Мої лінки carries a QR button that reveals an for that row; the code is never suffixed with .svg; the image is loaded by the browser as an image, never fetched and injected as inline

tasks.title: Tests: unit + integration for AC-01..05, AC-07
tasks.dod: npm run test:fast green; the media type is asserted as a prefix; the body is compared against renderQrSvg of the expected absolute short_url with the Host header pinned, and asserted different from renderQrSvg of the bare code; clicks are read before and after through GET /api/stats/:code; a rejected render yields 500 rather than a hung request; the AC-04 case is added only when link-expiry ships

tasks.title: Tests: e2e through the UI
tasks.dod: npm run test:e2e green; a real browser activates the QR button, the image is visible and decoded (naturalWidth > 0), and the row's click count is the same after the browser has actually fetched /api/qr/<code>
